title: Concept Sets
description: Concept Sets are curated collections of OMOP standard vocabulary concepts used to represent clinical entities such as conditions, drugs, measurements, and procedures. They serve as reusable building blocks within cohort definitions and analyses, ensuring consistent clinical logic across studies.
tips: Use the Vocabulary Browser to explore concept hierarchies and identify descendants before finalizing a concept set.
Enable 'Include Descendants' for condition and drug concepts to capture all coded variations within the OMOP hierarchy.
Use the Mapping Assistant to identify source codes that map to your target standard concepts, improving capture completeness.
Share concept sets across cohort definitions rather than duplicating logic to simplify future updates and ensure consistency.
Document the clinical rationale for included and excluded concepts to support peer review and reproducibility.
